rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARIE GIL VALDIVIA, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST VS. SOCO WEST, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. 1. Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for plaintiff Arnold Valdivia is GRANTED, subject to the following. Prior to the hearing, Brayton Purcell must file a proper proof of service identifying the addresses where Mr. Valdivia and all other plaintiffs were served by mail. No opposition filed. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on May 28, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: ROSEMARIE GIL VALDIVIA, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST VS. SOCO WEST, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2024, in Department 301, Line 1. 3. Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Olivia Valdivia, and minor plaintiffs Paul Valdivia Silva, Albert Valdivia Silva, and Romeo Valdivia Silva is GRANTED, subject to the following. Prior to the hearing, Brayton Purcell must lodge four (4) separate proposed orders, one for each individual client, including the minor plaintiffs. In addition, Brayton Purcell must file a proper proof of service identifying the addresses where Ms. Valdivia, the minor plaintiffs, and all other plaintiffs were served by mail. No opposition filed. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on May 28, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. REED VS. AMCORD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, May 28, 2024, in Department 301, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Trial-Setting Preference Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 36(a), (d), and (e) is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed. 1. The trial date is September 16, 2024 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is September 25, 2024. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 10, 2024. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is August 30, 2024. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is September 13, 2024. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 28, 2024. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. 8. No later than June 11, 2024: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Tentative ruling continues in Part 2 of 2.)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. REED VS. AMCORD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF CAROL REED MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULING ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY.)
rulings.Rulings: (Part 2 of 2) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry) 9. A further case management conference is set for July 30, 2024 at 1:30 p.m. a. Case management conference statements are not required. b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom. c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing. 10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than August 5, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due August 19, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on August 12, 2024. 11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, September 9, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. The prevailing party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 301 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on May 28, 2024 at 9:30 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (301/RCE)
